Hero.title: Git Genie
Hero.subtitle: AI-powered Git assistant
Hero.product_hunt_alt: GitGenie - Powered git assistant | Product Hunt

Features.title: Features
Features.f1_body: Stage, commit, push, and merge in one go.

HowItWorks.title: How it works
HowItWorks.subtitle: Just 4 steps to streamline your commit workflow. From installation to pushing production-ready commits, Git Genie handles the heavy lifting with AI-powered Conventional Commits.
HowItWorks.s1_desc: Get started by installing Git Genie globally or locally in your project. One command is all you need.
HowItWorks.s2_desc: Add your Gemini API key once. It's stored securely in ~/.gitgenie/config.json for future use.
HowItWorks.s3_desc: Describe your changes naturally. Git Genie generates Conventional Commits using AI in seconds.
HowItWorks.s4_desc: Finalize and push to your branch or auto-merge to main. Streamline your entire workflow.

Usage.title: Usage & Flags
Usage.subtitle: Configure Git Genie behavior with clean, human-friendly flags.
Usage.shortcuts_subtitle: Manage your branches effortlessly with these shortcuts.
Usage.s1: Create and switch to a new branch.
Usage.s3: Create a worktree for a branch.

FAQ.title: Frequently asked questions
FAQ.subtitle: Everything you need to know about GitGenie.

items.question: Is my API key secure?
items.answer: Yes. Your API key is stored securely using your operating system's keychain where available. As a fallback, it uses an encrypted local configuration file. Keys never leave your machine except to authenticate requests with Google.

items.question: Does GitGenie read my entire codebase?
items.answer: No. GitGenie only analyzes the diff of your currently staged files to generate context-aware commit messages. Your full source code remains local and is not uploaded.

items.question: Which AI model powers the commit generation?
items.answer: GitGenie uses Google's Gemini model for AI commit generation, optimized for fast CLI feedback and professional Conventional Commits.

items.question: Does it work on Windows?
items.answer: Yes. GitGenie is built on Node.js and works on Windows, macOS, and Linux, including PowerShell, Command Prompt, and WSL.

items.question: What is the --osc flag?
items.answer: The Open Source Contribution (--osc) flag helps format branch names for contributions. It prompts for an issue number and creates a branch name that follows common project contribution patterns.

items.question: Can I use it with any Git repository?
items.answer: Yes. GitGenie works with any Git repository. If you run it in a folder that is not a Git repo yet, it can initialize one for you.

NewAddOns.title: New Add Ons
NewAddOns.body: Intelligently split mixed changes into clean, logical commits.

getting_started: > **Essence:** `gg` automates staging, Conventional Commit creation, branch flow, optional merge to main, and push. It also includes secure API key storage and AI-powered branch/PR naming.
Configure your Gemini API key once:
Make a commit with AI assist:
gg "add user profile section" --type feat --scope ui --genie
Priority order used by the CLI:
commands: `<desc>` is a short human summary used for the fallback commit message if AI is unavailable.
- `config <apikey>` — securely store your Gemini API key.
- `--no-branch` — commit directly to main and skip branch prompt.
- `--push-to-main` — merge current branch into main and push.
- `--remote <url>` — add remote origin for a new repository.
Run `gg` without enough context to open the interactive command palette. Use arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, and Esc or Ctrl+C to cancel.
workflows: 2. Check whether the current folder is a Git repository.
3. Add a remote when `--remote` is provided.
5. Handle branch selection, creation, or open source mode.
6. Stage files when no staged diff exists.
7. Generate an AI commit message when `--genie` and a key are available.
9. Push, merge, or clean up branches depending on flags.
Initialize a new repo with remote:
ai: Git Genie uses Gemini to generate Conventional Commit messages from the staged diff.
The prompt asks for an imperative, concise, lowercase Conventional Commit without a trailing period.
Only staged diff data is sent. Untracked files and unstaged content are not included.
- Branch names use a descriptive kebab-case format.
- Open source mode can include an issue number.
- PR titles are generated from branch changes and commit history where supported.
If AI fails or no key is configured, Git Genie falls back to a normal commit message.
new_add_ons: Extend Git Genie with optional capabilities that improve commit hygiene and developer workflow efficiency.
`gg split` analyzes mixed changes and helps break them into clean, logical commits.
- Interactive review lets you confirm commit boundaries.
open_source: Use the `--osc` flag for open source workflows.
Git Genie prompts for an issue number and creates a branch name like:
With `--genie`, the short title can be generated by AI:
This keeps branches organized for project maintainers and reviewers.
reference: gg "add dark mode toggle" --type feat --scope ui
Force AI when a key is available:
Add a remote in the same run:
Open source branch with an issue:
- Missing API key: run `gg config <key>` or export `GEMINI_API_KEY`.
- No changes detected: check `git status` and `.gitignore`.
- Push fails: verify remote URL and authentication.
- Merge conflicts: resolve manually, then commit and push.
security: - API keys are stored in the OS keychain when available.
- The encrypted fallback is stored in `~/.gitgenie/config.json`.
- Keys are never hardcoded in source.
- Only staged diff data is sent for AI generation.
- Avoid staging secrets and keep `.env` files in `.gitignore`.
**How do I rotate API keys?** Run `gg config <new_key>`.
**What if I hit quota?** Configure a backup key with `gg config <backup_key>`.
**Does it support Windows?** Yes, PowerShell and Windows Credential Vault are supported.
